pr.title: lb: improve fn_80027488 matching, tidy a few header signatures
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2507 made a small lb library matching/header cleanup. In `fn_80027488`, it replaced accesses through `lbl_80433710.x194` and `.x274` with direct extern array references `lbl_804338A4` and `lbl_80433984`, and changed local declaration order so MWCC materializes addresses in the desired order. It also replaced several lb header `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders with explicit `void` signatures already used by the C files. The PR body and bot report record fuzzy progress for `fn_80027488` from 89.55% to 94.86%, but no 100% function match.

key_files.role: Adjusted `fn_80027488` pointer initialization to use standalone extern arrays and changed local declaration order for MWCC address materialization.
Replaced two `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with explicit `void` signatures.
Replaced four `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with explicit `void` signatures.
Replaced four header declarations using unknown placeholders with explicit `void` parameter lists or return types.

what_changed.change: `fn_80027488` now initializes `b` from `lbl_80433984` and `a` from `lbl_804338A4` instead of taking fields from `lbl_80433710`.
Local declaration order in `fn_80027488` changed from `a` then `b` to `b` then `a`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says declaration order was chosen for MWCC address materialization

what_changed.change: `lb_8000FA94`, `lb_80014534`, `lb_8001955C`, `fn_800195FC`, `lb_800198E0`, `lb_80019900`, `lb_8001C600`, `lb_8001C658`, `lb_8001CF18`, and `lb_8001D1F4` had unknown prototype placeholders replaced with explicit signatures visible in the diff.
The PR reports fuzzy score improvement for `fn_80027488` from 89.55% to 94.86%, not a 100% match.

smart_moves.lesson: When symbol metadata indicates standalone arrays at the same addresses as struct fields, direct extern references can improve codegen and review clarity over routing through a broader struct field access.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says arrays live at exactly those addresses per `symbols.txt`
raw_diff: field accesses replaced by direct array names

smart_moves.lesson: Header cleanup can be bundled as compile-time no-op when unknown macros already expand to the same ABI, but the PR evidence only supports this for the listed lb signatures.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says `.c` files already use actual `void` signatures and macros expand to `void`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local declaration order can affect MWCC register/address materialization; in this PR, declaring `b` before `a` was used to improve `fn_80027488` fuzzy matching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary and Functions matched sections

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not call a fuzzy-score improvement a full match: this PR explicitly records progress to 94.86% and documents the remaining prologue mismatch.

When a body already proves a no-argument `void` signature, replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` in the owning header improves header truthfulness without changing ABI, if the macro expansion claim is verified for that case.

naming_conventions.convention: This PR preserves address-style global names such as `lbl_804338A4`, `lbl_80433984`, and `lbl_80433710` rather than inventing semantic names.
Recovered function prototypes keep existing address-style function names such as `lb_8001955C` and `fn_800195FC`; the cleanup changes signatures, not symbol names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use direct extern array references instead of struct-field accesses when the symbols are known to live at the exact accessed addresses.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.effect: Reported to improve `fn_80027488` fuzzy score from 89.55% to 94.86%.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary and Functions matched sections

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reorder local declarations to influence MWCC address materialization order.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.effect: The PR says declaring `b` before `a` makes MWCC materialize addresses in the order the original expects, though one prologue instruction mismatch remains.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary and Functions matched sections

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Track remaining mismatch explicitly when a score improves but does not close.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.effect: The PR records the remaining `lis r3/lis r3` versus `lis r4/lis r3` prologue difference.

review_feedback.feedback: decomp-dev bot reported one improvement in unmatched item `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` / `fn_80027488`, bytes `+23`, before 89.55%, after 94.86%.
No human review comments or formal reviews were loaded in this PR slice.

notes: Changed files and exact diff hunks are fully loaded.
Fuzzy score improvement is supported by both PR body and decomp-dev bot report.
The claim that `lbl_804338A4` and `lbl_80433984` live at exactly the struct-field addresses is from the PR author body; `symbols.txt` itself was not loaded in this slice.
Build verification and no-regression claim are PR-author reported; no independent build log is loaded beyond the body text.
No human review comments are present in the loaded slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Declaration order can influence MWCC address materialization
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2507 used local declaration order in `fn_80027488` (`b` before `a`) to make MWCC materialize addresses closer to the original. This improved fuzzy matching but did not produce a 100% match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Replace unknown prototype placeholders when C bodies prove void signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2507 replaced several lb header `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders with explicit `void` signatures that the PR body says were already used by the corresponding C files and were compile-time no-ops.

source_update_candidates.title: Record declaration-order steering as a narrow MWCC matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2507 is evidence that changing local declaration order can affect MWCC address materialization and fuzzy matching. If added to standards, keep it narrow and require objdiff/build evidence because this PR improved but did not fully match `fn_80027488`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: declares `b` before `a` so MWCC materializes addresses in original order
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces the existing loaded standard that declaration order changes are matching tactics needing evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: lb headers in PR #2507 had unknown placeholders replaced by explicit void signatures
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changed selected declarations in `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.h`, `src/melee/lb/lb_0192.h`, and `src/melee/lb/lbcardgame.h` from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to explicit `void` signatures.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path-specific cleanup fact, but should be curated against current source before adding.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that direct extern array references are always preferable to struct fields; the PR evidence is limited to `fn_80027488` and the author's symbols.txt address claim.
Do not record `fn_80027488` as matched; the PR explicitly says no 100% matches and reports only fuzzy improvement.
Do not infer semantic names for `lbl_804338A4`, `lbl_80433984`, or `lbl_80433710`; the PR kept address-style names.
